summary: Test suite has solid unit test coverage of all core requirements (parsing, license lookup, compliance checking, report generation, error handling) with good use of pytest fixtures and mocking. However, several edge cases are untested: empty manifests, missing config keys, scoped packages, case sensitivity, and all-approved/denied scenarios. The integration test harness (run_tests.py) is brittle — it parses act output directly with magic strings like '9 passed' and 'APPROVED: express@^4.18.0', making it fragile to output format changes. The harness also depends on external files (workflow, test_fixtures, .actrc) and assumes specific workflow behavior without explicit verification. Unit tests would catch most regressions in core functionality, but the brittleness of the integration layer and missing edge case coverage reduce overall confidence.
